§6P2P Tunnels§f:
The §6P2P Tunnels §f(appliedenergistics2:part:460) are §ecable parts §f that §btransparently connect §f any two networks of compatible types. They are the §6power-distance §f and §6cable-extension §f solution for AE2 networks.

§6Production Path (network/parts/tunnels_me.json)§f:
Pattern " a / aba / ccc":
- §ba§f: §6Iron Ingot §f(ingotIron) — 4 (top + middle corners)
- §bb§f: §6Engineering Processor §f(appliedenergistics2:material:24) — 1 (middle centre)
- §cc§f: §6Fluix Crystal §f(any crystal in the §6#appliedenergistics2:fluixCrystal §foredict tag) — 3 (bottom row)
- Yields 1× ME P2P Tunnel

§6P2P Tunnel Family§f:
- §bME P2P Tunnel §f(appliedenergistics2:part:460) — connects two §6ME networks §f
- §bItem P2P Tunnel §f(variants) — connect §6item §f I/O
- §bFluid P2P Tunnel §f(variants) — connect §6fluid §f I/O
- §bRedstone P2P Tunnel §f — relays §bredstone signals §f
- §bFE P2P Tunnel §f — relays §6Forge Energy §f (RF/Tesla)
- §bEU P2P Tunnel §f — relays §6EU §f (IC2)
- §bLight P2P Tunnel §f — relays §6light updates §f

§6Function (PartP2PTunnelME.java:53-225)§f:
- §bNetwork bridging §f: place two P2P tunnels of the same type on §bseparate §fME networks; they automatically pair
- §bTransparent transport §f: items, fluids, power, and redstone flow through the tunnels as if the networks were directly connected
- §bCompressed channel §f: an ME P2P Tunnel uses §b1 channel §f on each side; with §6Dense Cable §f, it carries §b32 channels §f of payload
- §bTunnel cost §f: each P2P tunnel has an §bouter proxy §f and an §binner proxy §f (PartP2PTunnelME.java:67-68), consuming §bcable channels §f
- §bFrequency §f: each tunnel has §b1-5 §f frequency slots; matching frequencies pair up

§6Direct Descendants (TaskRetrieval graph)§f:
The jei-graph shows 0 items the P2P Tunnel is a component of, but it is referenced in 1,250 §6use sites §f across the mod pack. Common uses:
- §bLong-distance networks §f: place one P2P on each end of a long cable run
- §bWireless extension §f: pair P2P with a §6Wireless Access Point §f for cross-network routing
- §bMultinetwork setups §f: P2P is the only way to have two §6logically separate §f networks exchange items

§6Practical Use§f:
- §eTip§f: number your P2P tunnels with §6coloured dyes §f to keep track of pairings
- §eTip§f: a P2P tunnel consumes §b0 AE/t §f at idle, but its endpoints do consume channels from the parent cables
- §cWarning§f: P2P tunnels do §cnot §f work across §bchunk loads §f unless both ends are in §6loaded §f chunks
- §cWarning§f: an ME P2P tunnel on a §cbroken §f network will §cpair §f to the §bnext available §f tunnel of the same type — be careful with multi-network setups
